description: Evals for the GroundX web UI skill. Each test prompt exercises a different surface area (page-level layout, modal/form, settings list). The rubric checks that the generated code follows the canonical tokens, wrappers, and corrections — not just that it compiles. The skill is expected to read ../groundx-design-standards/references/ for brand invariants.

tests.prompt: Build an API Keys management page for the GroundX dashboard. It should show a list of the user's existing keys in a table (columns: Label, Key Prefix, Created, Last Used, Actions) with a '+ NEW KEY' pill button in the top-right of the section header. Each row has a copy-to-clipboard icon for the prefix and a delete icon. Include an empty state for when the user has no keys yet. Use the design system.

must_use: GxCard as the outer surface (not MUI Card or inline Box with border sx)
GxSectionHeader for the 'API KEYS' row with the + NEW KEY action on the right
CommonSubmitButton with startIcon={<AddIcon />} and borderRadius: BORDER_RADIUS_4X for the pill CTA
Named constants from '@/constants' — CORAL_ORANGE, LIGHT_GREY_2, BORDER_RADIUS_3X, PADDING — no hex literals
MUI IconButton with aria-label for copy and delete actions
fontSize='small' on icons inside table rows
Responsive behavior via theme.breakpoints or sx responsive objects
must_not_use: Hardcoded hex colors anywhere in JSX or sx
textTransform: 'uppercase' on button labels (rely on CommonSubmitButton default or write literal uppercase strings)
fontSize: '16px' or numeric fontSize on icons
bonus: Empty state uses the pattern from examples/ExampleEmptyState.tsx
A11y: delete icon has an aria-label that includes the key's label

tests.prompt: Generate a modal dialog for inviting a teammate to the workspace. Fields: email (required, must be valid email), role (dropdown with Admin / Editor / Viewer), optional personal message (textarea). Footer has Cancel + Send Invite buttons. Use Formik + Yup. Match the GroundX design system.

must_use: MUI <Dialog> with PaperProps={{ sx: { borderRadius: BORDER_RADIUS_3X } }}
DialogTitle with a close IconButton (aria-label='close') as shown in examples/ExampleFormModal.tsx
CommonTextField for email and message
CommonCancelButton + CommonSubmitButton (in that order) in DialogActions
Formik + Yup schema with email().required() and role oneOf validation
fullWidth maxWidth='sm' on the Dialog
Named tokens from '@/constants' only
must_not_use: Cancel button with any accent color (must be grey/text)
bonus: Multiline TextField for the personal message
Role Select uses MUI <Select> with consistent styling
isSubmitting disables the submit button without extra styling

tests.prompt: Build a Billing & Plan settings page. Show: (1) current plan card with plan name, usage bars (pages ingested, queries this month) and an 'Upgrade to Premium' CTA; (2) a payment method section with the current card on file and an 'Update payment method' button; (3) an invoice history table with Date, Amount, Status (paid/failed/pending as pills), Download link. Use the design system.

must_use: GxUsageCard or a close analog for the plan usage section
gx-premium-button variant for the Upgrade CTA
GxPill with variant='success'|'warning'|'error' for invoice status
GxCard for each top-level section with Stack spacing={3} between them
GxSectionHeader for 'PAYMENT METHOD' and 'INVOICE HISTORY'
Responsive content padding: MAIN_CONTENT_PADDING at md+, smaller at xs
Icons from @mui/icons-material (DownloadIcon for invoice download)
must_not_use: A button styled into a status pill (use GxPill)
bonus: Failed invoices use ERROR_RED via GxPill variant='error'
Download icon is a properly labeled IconButton in the table row
Empty state if no invoices yet

sanity_checks.description: Files that must exist for the skill and its dependency to render correctly.

how_to_run: For each test prompt, run the agent in a session with both skills enabled, save the generated code, then grep for forbidden patterns: (a) '#' followed by a hex character inside .tsx files, (b) '@media (max-width', (c) 'boxShadow', (d) 'fontSize: "' on icons, (e) literal '<Card>' imports from '@mui/material'. Each hit fails a must_not_use criterion. Then inspect imports to confirm the must_use criteria. The sanity_checks can be automated with: for file in required_files; do test -f "$SKILL_DIR/$file"; done.
